experiment: M7 theta-sweep look-ahead LLM (frozen Qwen2.5-1.5B reads current-cell + theta-sweep look-ahead tokens, judges whether the path AHEAD is blocked in a NOVEL per-episode layout)
training: LoRA + gated fusion, 1600 steps, LR warmup 120; balanced binary (chance 0.50)

off_text_only._desc: all spatial tokens zeroed (cortex ablated) -> the causal ON/OFF

no_sweep._desc: current-cell tokens kept, sweep tokens zeroed -> isolates the look-ahead

shuffled._desc: sweep tokens sampled along a WRONG heading (look-ahead, wrong direction)

decisive_contrast: ON vs NO-SWEEP (both carry the cortex/current cell; only the theta-sweep tokens differ): +34%, p=0.0081
seed_variability: ON mean 0.82 with a wide 95% CI (+/-0.16): most seeds are well above chance (e.g. seed 7 = 0.79); the ON-vs-OFF p (0.030) being larger than the ON-vs-no-sweep p (0.008) implies a seed or two where the LoRA did not converge to reading the sweep (ON near chance), which widens the CI. Every seed still has ON >= each ablation.
headline: A frozen LLM judges whether the path AHEAD is blocked in a NOVEL layout ONLY when given the theta-sweep look-ahead tokens: ON 82% vs text-only-OFF 50%, sweep-ablated 48%, wrong-heading-shuffled 56% (chance 50%). Removing the sweep collapses it to chance (48-50%); mis-directing it (wrong heading) drops it to near chance (56%) -- either way far below the 82% with the real sweep. The Vollan look-around, made load-bearing for language.
